findings.title: Sunset revenue model decoded — bank interest, not user fees
findings.description: Sunset (sunsetapp.com) is completely free to families. Revenue model: earns fees from partner banks on interest generated while estate funds sit in FDIC-insured estate account (up to $3M). New 2026 features: e-notarization, RUFADAA-compliant digital asset/crypto discovery, debt/liability identification (credit cards, mortgages, property taxes, personal loans), 1-day asset discovery. Families keep 100% of discovered assets. SOC 2 Type II certified. iPhone + Android native apps. Narrowly focused on asset discovery/transfer/consolidation, NOT the full emotional checklist experience.
findings.suggested_action: Update Sunset competitive intelligence in CLAUDE.md. AfterLoss differentiates on: (1) web-first no-download, (2) empathy-first full-lifecycle guidance (not just assets), (3) AI document generation, (4) phone scripts library, (5) state probate education. Sunset is strong on the financial/asset side but weak on the emotional/administrative guidance side.

findings.title: SwiftProbate confirmed 3,200+ county-level SEO pages with 2026 updates
findings.description: SwiftProbate (swiftprobate.com) has county-level probate guides for nearly every US county, all updated with '(2026)' in titles. URL pattern: /probate/{state}/{county-name}. Each page includes: courthouse locations, filing fees, local rules, small estate options, step-by-step process. Also running aggressive comparison pages: /compare/elayne, /compare/eversettled, /compare/estateexec, /compare/clearestate. Probate checklist blog post: /blog/probate-checklist-step-by-step. Price: $39 one-time.
findings.suggested_action: SwiftProbate's comparison pages are intercepting competitor-seeking traffic. AfterLoss should plan its own comparison page strategy (e.g., /compare/sunset, /compare/atticus, /compare/swiftprobate) as a P3 SEO initiative. County-level pages already tracked as issue #266.

findings.title: Elayne confirmed free tier with account requirement + aggressive SEO content
findings.description: Elayne (elayne.com) free tier includes: family collaboration, document sharing, estate plan management. Paid tier: $250 for full account discovery + human support. Running date-stamped content strategy with monthly guides (e.g., 'Delete Cash App Account Permanently March 2026', 'Transfer on Death Deed Guide February 2026'). Has comparison content reviewing competitors (Alix). SOC 2 Type II + HIPAA compliant. Free tier still REQUIRES an account. AfterLoss differentiates on no-account-required.
findings.suggested_action: Elayne's date-stamped content strategy is clever — each monthly post targets fresh keywords. AfterLoss should consider similar date-stamped guides (e.g., 'Probate Process [State] 2026') for state pages. But core differentiator remains: no account required + AI doc gen.

findings.title: Atticus iOS-only with mixed App Store reviews — web gap remains wide open
findings.description: Atticus (weareatticus.com) is iOS-only, ~$15/month subscription. App Store reviews mixed: some users praise the step-by-step guide, others report bugs with the reports feature and unresponsive support. Trustpilot reviews (March 2026) mostly positive. Fast Company World Changing Idea winner. Has well-ranking article: 'What To Do When Someone Dies: The 2026 Checklist'. Critical gap: NO web app, NO Android app. AfterLoss's web-first approach is a major differentiator for the 50%+ of users on Android or desktop.
findings.suggested_action: Atticus's content marketing (2026 Checklist article) is ranking well. AfterLoss needs to publish its own definitive '2026 Checklist' content page optimized for this keyword. Web-first + free is a strong counter-position.

findings.title: Empathy launched Leave Support with MetLife (Oct 2025) — expanding B2B scope
findings.description: Empathy and MetLife launched Leave Support (October 2025), a digital platform for employees navigating bereavement leave. This expands Empathy's B2B insurance carrier model. Partners now include: MetLife, New York Life, Allianz, Aflac, TIAA, MassMutual, Prudential, AT&T, Sun Life. Assigns 1:1 Care Manager. Saves families average 200 hours. Still pure B2B — not accessible to families without carrier partnership.
findings.suggested_action: Empathy's B2B lock-in means they serve only policyholders of partner companies. The vast majority of grieving families (those without MetLife/partner policies) have no access. This is AfterLoss's market — direct-to-consumer, anyone via Google.

findings.title: EverSettled pricing may be $199 base, not $1,499 — verify
findings.description: Web research shows EverSettled pricing at $199 with optional specialist support. The $1,499 figure in CLAUDE.md may be outdated or represent a premium full-service tier. SwiftProbate's comparison page lists EverSettled at $199. The EverSettled website mentions a free 2-week trial and 500+ families served. San Francisco based.
findings.suggested_action: Update EverSettled pricing in CLAUDE.md to $199 base with optional specialist support at higher tiers. The $1,499 figure may have been a temporary or premium tier.

findings.title: Michigan small estate threshold: $53,000 for 2026 (NEW — not in CLAUDE.md)
findings.description: Michigan's small estate threshold for deaths in 2026 is $53,000 (cost-of-living adjusted annually). Real property with mortgage/lien: can subtract up to $264,000 from property value. Michigan Legal Help (michiganlegalhelp.org) is the authoritative source. This is a NEW state data point not currently tracked in CLAUDE.md.
findings.suggested_action: Add Michigan to the Key State Probate Threshold Changes section in CLAUDE.md. This data point is important for issue #253 (50-state probate rules database).

findings.title: Justia 50-State Survey is THE key data source for probate rules curation
findings.description: Justia's Small Estates Laws and Procedures 50-State Survey (justia.com/probate/probate-administration/small-estates/small-estates-laws-and-procedures-50-state-survey/) provides comprehensive data on small estate thresholds, simplified procedures, and requirements for every state. This should be the primary starting point for issue #253 data curation, supplemented by individual state court websites for verification.
findings.suggested_action: Reference Justia survey as the starting data source in issue #253. Cross-reference with Nolo state guides and individual state court websites.

findings.title: SSA-721 form updated (10-2024 edition) — still no online filing for families
findings.description: Form SSA-721 has an updated edition (10-2024). Still only submittable by funeral directors or by phone. The SSA website now has a cleaner guide at ssa.gov/personal-record/when-someone-dies. USAGov also provides reporting guidance at usa.gov/social-security-report-a-death. Electronic Death Registration is available to funeral homes through state vital statistics offices.
findings.suggested_action: Update phone scripts content to reference the current SSA-721 form edition. Include both SSA direct page and USAGov page as resources in the checklist.

findings.title: Top SERP competitors for head terms are informational articles, not interactive tools
findings.description: SERP analysis for 'what to do when someone dies checklist' (March 2026): AARP, Atticus Magazine, Ever Loved, Fidelity, Dignity Memorial, Kiplinger, Trust & Will, Legal Voice, Bankers Life. ALL are static articles/checklists. None are interactive tools. AfterLoss's interactive checklist with AI doc gen would be genuinely differentiated in search results. Rich snippets (FAQ schema, HowTo schema) could drive CTR above static articles.
findings.suggested_action: SEO infrastructure (#261) should include JSON-LD structured data for FAQ, HowTo, and Article schemas. Add FAQ schema to state pages for rich snippets. Interactive tool pages should use HowTo schema.

findings.title: Date-stamped content strategy — competitors using monthly freshness signals
findings.description: Both Elayne and Atticus publish date-stamped content ('February 2026 guide', '2026 Checklist'). SwiftProbate includes '(2026)' in all county page titles. Google rewards freshness for YMYL (Your Money Your Life) queries like estate planning. AfterLoss state pages should include the current year in titles and be auto-updated annually.
findings.suggested_action: For issue #258 (programmatic state pages), include the year in page titles: 'Probate Process in [State] (2026 Guide)'. Use ISR to auto-regenerate pages with updated years. This is a key SEO freshness signal for YMYL content.

findings.title: Phone scripts library confirmed as unique differentiator — no competitor offers this
findings.description: Extensive web research confirms NO competitor offers pre-written phone scripts for calling SSA, banks, insurance companies, or subscription services after a death. Everplans has the most comprehensive written guide but no scripts. The phone call is the #1 anxiety point for grieving families. Scripts should include: what to say, what information to have ready, what to expect from the agent, what to do if denied.
findings.suggested_action: Issue #265 (phone scripts library) is correctly prioritized as P1. This should be a prominent feature in marketing and SEO content. Target keywords: 'what to say when calling SSA about a death', 'how to call bank about deceased account holder', 'cancel subscription after death what to say'.

findings.title: Subscription cancellation templates — expanding to 150+ services with categorization
findings.description: Everplans and funeral.com have the most comprehensive lists of accounts to cancel after death (100-150 categories). Key categories: streaming (Netflix, Hulu, Disney+, etc.), social media (Facebook memorial, Instagram, Twitter/X), financial (banks, credit cards, investment accounts), utilities, insurance, memberships (gym, warehouse clubs), medical, email/cloud storage. Each has different processes: some online, some phone-only, some require death certificate.
findings.suggested_action: Issue #256 should target 150+ services organized by category. Include for each: company name, cancellation method (online/phone/mail), required documents, expected timeline, refund policy for prepaid services.

findings.title: Bereavement UX design principles validated by research
findings.description: Key validated principles: (1) One question per screen with clear next steps, (2) Soft unobtrusive progress indicator, (3) No pressure to finish — 'Take your time' messaging, (4) Progressive disclosure — don't overwhelm, (5) Cultural flexibility — avoid Western-centric assumptions, (6) Templates reduce decision fatigue, (7) Allow pause/resume — grief is nonlinear, (8) User controls who sees what (privacy), (9) Meaningful defaults. Reference article: 'Designing for Death: A UX Guide for End-of-Life Products' by Matthew Stephens on Medium.
findings.suggested_action: Add bereavement UX design principles to CLAUDE.md for issue #255 (landing page redesign) and #254 (checklist engine). The 'one question per screen' pattern should guide the onboarding flow (#259).

findings.title: Comparison page SEO strategy — intercept competitor-seeking traffic
findings.description: SwiftProbate runs comparison pages (/compare/elayne, /compare/eversettled, etc.) that intercept users searching for competitor reviews. This is a proven SaaS SEO strategy. AfterLoss could target: 'Sunset app review', 'Atticus vs AfterLoss', 'free estate settlement app'. Since AfterLoss is 100% free, comparison pages would consistently win on pricing.
findings.suggested_action: Create a new P3 GitHub issue for comparison page SEO strategy. Build /compare/{competitor} pages for Sunset, Atticus, SwiftProbate, Elayne, EverSettled. Emphasize: free, no account required, web-first, AI doc gen.

findings.title: P0 scaffold violations (#252) still open — blocks all feature work
findings.description: Issue #252 documents 13 scaffold violations from PR #213 reviews. All documented in learnings.md as recurring patterns. Key fixes: suppressHydrationWarning on date, remove font-family override, expand CSS vars, beforeEach in tests, add /api/health, add Prettier, ESLint data-testid rule, env.ts error tests. This is P0 and blocks all feature development.
findings.suggested_action: Assign issue #252 to the next implementation worker. All 13 acceptance criteria are mechanical — should be a clean single-PR fix.

findings.title: Only 12 total tests (9 unit + 4 E2E) — critical coverage gap
findings.description: AfterLoss has 3 test files with 12 total tests. All are smoke tests for the landing page. No tests for onboarding, checklist, document generation, state guides, or any business logic. As features are built, test coverage must grow proportionally.
findings.suggested_action: Each feature issue (#254, #259, #260, etc.) should require corresponding tests in acceptance criteria. E2E tests should cover the full user flow: landing → onboarding → checklist → document generation → download.

findings.title: CI/CD pipeline not yet created — issue #127 open
findings.description: No CI/CD workflow exists for AfterLoss (ci-afterloss.yml). Other products like ScoreRebound have deploy workflows and E2E test workflows. AfterLoss needs at minimum: build + lint + test on PR, deploy to Vercel on merge to main, E2E smoke tests post-deploy.
findings.suggested_action: Issue #127 covers CI/CD. Should be prioritized after #252 (scaffold fixes) and before heavy feature work, so all subsequent PRs get proper CI validation.

stack_recommendation.rationale: Current stack is correct. Next.js 15 App Router with SSG/ISR for SEO-critical state pages, Supabase for optional user progress sync, Vercel for fast CDN delivery. No stack changes needed. Key additions needed: zustand for localStorage persistence, @anthropic-ai/sdk for doc gen, @react-pdf/renderer for PDF output (client-side only). generateStaticParams() confirmed as the right approach for programmatic SEO pages.

apis_discovered.name: SSA Death Notification
apis_discovered.notes: NO public API. Deaths reported by phone (800-772-1213) or in person. Funeral homes use EDR. Form SSA-721 (10-2024 edition). USAGov guide: usa.gov/social-security-report-a-death

apis_discovered.name: VitalChek Death Certificates
apis_discovered.notes: NO public API. Consumer ordering only. Exclusive partner of 450+ governing agencies. Costs $5-$32/copy. 3-10 day processing. Uses LexisNexis for identity verification. Potential affiliate opportunity.

apis_discovered.name: IRS Estate Forms
apis_discovered.notes: Form 1041 (estate income, $600+ threshold, due April 15). Form 706 (estate tax, >$15M permanent threshold, due 9 months after death). Form 4768 (6-month extension). Portability: MUST file 706 even if under threshold.

apis_discovered.name: Justia 50-State Small Estate Survey
apis_discovered.notes: PRIMARY data source for issue #253. Covers all states' small estate procedures, thresholds, and requirements.

apis_discovered.name: Nolo State Probate Guides
apis_discovered.notes: State-by-state probate guides. Also has 15% affiliate program with 120-day cookie. Books like 'How to Probate an Estate in California' ($42.99).

apis_discovered.name: Michigan Legal Help — Small Estate
apis_discovered.notes: NEW: Michigan threshold $53,000 for 2026. Mortgage/lien deduction up to $264,000. Authoritative state source.

apis_discovered.name: Rocket Lawyer Affiliate
apis_discovered.notes: 30% commission via Yazing/VigLink. 30-day cookie. Highest legal affiliate commission. Free to join.

apis_discovered.name: Trust & Will Affiliate
apis_discovered.notes: 20% commission via Impact.com. Avg $80/sale, up to $180/transaction. 30-day cookie.

apis_discovered.name: LegalZoom Affiliate
apis_discovered.notes: 15% commission via CJ Affiliate. 30-day cookie. $50 min payout.

apis_discovered.name: Nolo Affiliate
apis_discovered.notes: 15% commission. 120-day cookie — ideal for grief users who delay purchases. Via MyAffPro/Kolimbo. $25 min payout.

apis_discovered.name: BetterHelp Affiliate
apis_discovered.notes: $10-$150 per referral via Impact/ShareASale. Commissions scale with quality/volume. Grief counseling specialty available.
